title: Handling and sexing rabbits
subtitle: Picking up, holding, and determining the sex of rabbits safely at any age.
excerpt: A rabbit dropped from handling height can fracture its spine when it kicks and lands badly. Correct handling holds the animal securely so it cannot push off and fall, and distributes the weight along the body rather than suspending it from the scruff alone. Sexing at weaning prevents accidental litters in the grow-on pen.
sourceNotes:
RSPCA, Welfare standards for rabbits (2019). https://www.rspca.org.uk
British Rabbit Council, handling guidance. https://www.thebrc.org

content.text: Never lift a rabbit by the ears or by the

alone. The correct grip is one hand under the chest behind the front legs, the other supporting the hindquarters. The rabbit is held against your body with its head tucked into your elbow. This keeps the spine straight and prevents the powerful kick that causes spinal fracture if the animal is held at arm's length.
Approaching a rabbit in the cage

Approach from the front rather than reaching from above. A hand from above triggers a hawk-predator response. Place one hand flat in the cage and let the rabbit sniff it before moving to pick up. For a doe with a young litter, check the kits first from above and avoid reaching into the nest box unless necessary: disturbing the doe excessively in the first week can lead to abandonment.

Rabbits should be sexed and separated into buck and doe groups at weaning (four to five weeks). Hold the rabbit securely with one hand supporting the body. Gently press the skin on either side of the genital opening with thumb and forefinger: in a buck, the opening is circular and the penis protrudes as a small rounded nub with gentle pressure; in a doe, the opening is a slit with a small protrusion of vulval tissue. Both sexes look similar until weaning, so recheck at eight weeks to catch any errors.

attrs.body: Sexing before four weeks is unreliable because the genitalia of both sexes look very similar in newborns. If grow-on pens are too small to separate male and female kits cleanly, err toward more space and keep litter siblings together until eight weeks, when the difference is clear. Does can become pregnant at ten weeks; bucks are fertile from twelve weeks. Separate before these ages.
attrs.title: Age to reliable sexing

content.text: (rabbit on its back) induces stillness but the stillness is a fear response, not relaxation. Some veterinary procedures use it briefly. It is not a recommended way to sex or examine rabbits routinely because the stress response is significant.
